directive: Remove the driver abstraction layer added in round 2. Revert to direct use of the @pagerduty/pdjs API in the test (as in the original round 1 submission). Add a single code comment at the top of pagerduty-e2e.test.ts: this test is intentionally coupled to @pagerduty/pdjs — it validates library integration, not an abstract dispatch interface; if the library is swapped, this test is expected to be rewritten. No other changes. All three acceptance criteria (staging incident creation/resolution, retry injection, severity-tier payloads) must remain exercised.
rationale: Two Reviewer rejections, both warnings, both offering a resolution path. Round 2 presents two acceptable options: neutral domain interface (go all the way) or drop abstraction and own the coupling explicitly. Option A introduces abstraction for a concern the Reviewer themselves explicitly scoped out of this project ('which this project explicitly DOES NOT do'). A neutral domain interface that abstracts away the phase-1 library also undermines the test's purpose: this is an integration test whose job is to validate the specific library wired in phase 1. Coupling to that library's API is correct and expected here. Option B (drop abstraction, own the coupling with a comment) satisfies all three acceptance criteria, adds no speculative infrastructure, and is honest about the trade-off for future maintainers. The Reviewer finds both paths acceptable in Round 2; this directive takes Option B.
